Items.CustomText:
Elemental kibble. Strange stuff.

The guardians will take it -- the fire, the deep, the storm. Bring it to them. Bind them to your hand.

What falls to your blade can rise as your mount.

- S.

The deep watcher dies in Lemnokis.

Fire burns in Therokis. Storm rages over Pyranthos.

Their kind takes only elemental kibble — raise a ramshackle lootboss to mastercraft for it.

- S.

The fire watcher falls in Therokis.

Deep in Lemnokis, storm in Pyranthos still stand.

To tame their kind: evolve a ramshackle lootboss to mastercraft. Elemental kibble drops there.

- S.

Lightning ends over Pyranthos. The watcher is silent.

Fire in Therokis, deep in Lemnokis — both still stand.

Elemental kibble tames them. Raise a ramshackle lootboss to mastercraft.

- S.

Hunt the Savage Boss. Tame the snail that remains.

Feed it berries — it yields Slime Balls. All kibble above the weakest demands them.

- S.

The Cyclops falls. Necrotic kibble opens to you.

Among the Necrotic lands, Queen Bees bring honey to those who claim them. And the Golem awaits.

- S.

Ground silences thunder, but roots crack stone.

Raise its kind to ground the Storm Colossus. Beware the Nature — green things split the rock.

- S.

Cold kills what grows, but fire is older than cold.

Raise its kind to still the Nature Colossus. Beware the Volcanic — heat ends winter.

- S.

Water drowns the flame, but the storm boils the sea.

Raise its kind to extinguish the Volcanic Colossus. Beware the Storm — the lightning runs through every drop.

- S.

Roots break stone in time, but frost halts the bloom.

Raise its kind to fell the Earth Colossus. Beware the Frost — cold withers every leaf.

- S.

Fire claims the ice, but water claims the fire.

Raise its kind to melt the Frost Colossus. Beware the Hydro — depths smother every flame.

- S.

Lightning splits the sky, but the ground holds it down.

Raise its kind to bring the Hydro Colossus low. Beware the Earth — it answers to nothing above.

- S.

The Savage Achatina is not the only trial.

The Alpha Jerboa stalks the safer lands where the weak still cling to life. Find it. Kill it. Then the skeleton that rises.

- S.

Three kings rule these lands swamp, plains, desert.

All must fall to evolve the snail. Then the Cyclops Troll awaits.

- S.

Swamp. Plains. Desert. Three kings, three souls.

Bring them to your snail it will evolve, and its slime will fuel what comes next.

- S.

With three souls gathered, the snail transforms.

Then seek the Cyclops Troll. It is no mere beast a tyrant cyclops, bone-white and roaring. Strike it down.

- S.

The Golem breaks. Grenadier kibble is yours.

Fell five grenadiers with necrotics.

If the elemental path lies untouched, walk it. Skorvax and Zaphyros need both halves done.

- S.

Earth crumbles. Stone yields nothing eternal.

Fell every Colossus and Grenadier boss. Skorvax and Zaphyros remain — Prime opens only when they fall.

- S.

Even the wild thing dies in the end.

Clear all Colossi and Grenadier bosses. Skorvax and Zaphyros wait beyond — Prime is theirs to surrender.

- S.

The storm breaks against your blade.

Fell all Colossi and Grenadier bosses. Skorvax and Zaphyros are the next prey — Prime opens only when both lie still.

- S.

Fire dies as easily as it burns.

Clear all Colossi and Grenadier bosses. Then Skorvax and Zaphyros — only their fall opens Prime to you.

- S.

Even tides recede. Even oceans drain.

Clear every Colossus and Grenadier boss. Skorvax and Zaphyros wait at the threshold — Prime follows their fall.

- S.

Ice cracks. Cold cannot stand against fury.

Fell all Colossi and Grenadier bosses. Skorvax and Zaphyros remain — Prime is earned only when they fall.

- S.

Skorvax falls. The chitinous king bleeds black on the sand.

Warden Kibble opens to you. The machine god still slumbers — seek the Scion of Darkness. Its scrolls call older things, and Spirit and Chaos must answer before the gears wake.

- S.

Zaphyros falls. The shape it wore was never its own.

Warden Kibble opens to you. The machine god still slumbers — find the Scion of Darkness. What it heralds is the path. Spirit and Chaos must answer before the gears wake.

- S.

The Bionic God falls. Nuclear and Vampiric kibble unlock.

Origin creatures walk the world now. Hunt them. What they carry opens the next door — and the kibble of Wardens.

- S.

The Celestial Hydra falls. Light bleeds from its many heads.

Demonic Lucifer still walks the dark places. End it as well. Only then will the bench yield Draconis.

- S.

Demonic Lucifer falls. The dark hush settles.

The Celestial Hydra still walks the bright places. End it as well. Only then will the bench yield Draconis.

- S.
Draconis falls. The dragon's breath dies in its throat. What waited behind it does not breathe at all — the DarkGodking. The bench knows his summons now. Forge it when you dare. - S.

The Hellfire Boss falls. The fire goes back to where fire comes from.

Eleven Origins walk again. Older creatures. Older bones. Take a token from each. Draconis answers when the set is whole. - S.
Nemesis falls. The mask was thin. The bones you carried were a leash — the wrong end yours. The Juggernaut wakes now. The hunt is not over. - S.

The Terminator falls. Its voice was the grinding of metal teeth.

The SwordMaster still walks. End it too. Then hunt the Scion in the wild — its scrolls are the bench's last word.

- S.

The SwordMaster falls. Its blade kept the Bionic's sleep unbroken.

The Terminator still walks. End it too. Then find the Scion where it roams — what it carries opens the bench.

- S.

The Scion falls. Its scrolls scatter — names it was never meant to read.

One is the Bionic God. The bench will take it from here. Whatever wakes was waiting for the courier to die.

- S.

The Spirit Titan falls. Light bleeds out of the air where it stood.

Something hotter waits where the cold leaves. The Hellfire Boss tends a fire that was never his. Find him before he learns.- S.

The Chaos Titan unmakes itself in falling. The world steadies a moment.

That steadiness will burn. The Hellfire Boss keeps a flame the Titans were guarding. Take it from him.

- S.
The Knight falls. What he was guarding does not. The bench has held the Corrupted DarkGod Emperor's name longer than I've walked — and now it lets you read it. - S.

The Emperor's crown rusts where it falls.

Six godlies stir in his ending. Divinity has a smell. They carry it. Take what they leave behind. Nemesis sharpens itself on what you bring. - S.

The DarkGodking falls. His name was borrowed from older mouths.

The Nightmares walk now. Eleven kinds. Each one a piece of the Knight. Cut every piece down. He cannot sleep through that. - S.
